seo.title: Discuss your project | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: Send a concise overview of the project, product, or process. I will reply with the most sensible next step and an honest fit assessment.

page.title: Discuss your project | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Discuss your project and assess the fit
page.description: Send a concise overview of the project, product, or process. I will reply with the most sensible next step and an honest fit assessment.

hero.title: A short project summary is enough to assess the fit
hero.subtitle: You do not need a finished specification. The most useful inputs are the current problem, the target outcome, and the main delivery constraints.

intro: The first contact does not need to be heavy. A practical summary of what is not working, what should change, and whether this is a new build, takeover, internal tool, or integration project is usually enough.
If the fit is strong, I will suggest the next step. If it is not, I will say that directly rather than stretch out the process.
If you already know the timeline, team setup, current stack, or operational risk, that context helps shape the conversation faster.

sections.title: What is useful to include
body: The more concrete the context, the easier it is to recommend the right next step.
bullets: details about the current system or workflow

sections.title: How I usually respond
body: The response may suggest an introductory call, an app takeover review, a scoping phase, or a focused implementation step depending on the situation.

sections.title: Where the fit is strongest
body: The strongest fit is business web applications, internal tools, inherited app situations, and automation or integration work with real operational importance.

faq.question: Can I reach out without a detailed spec?
faq.answer: Yes. The detailed spec is not the starting requirement. Business context and intended outcome matter more at first.

faq.question: Do you handle urgent stabilisation or takeover work?
faq.answer: Yes. Existing-app situations often need a fast first step focused on risk reduction.

faq.question: Can we book a call directly?
faq.answer: Yes. You can book a time through Calendly from this page and from the thank-you page after submission.

faq.question: What if the project is not a good fit?
faq.answer: I will say so directly. The goal is clarity, not pushing an engagement that should not happen.

cta.label: Discuss your project
cta.note: A short project summary is enough to start the conversation.

props.heading: A short project summary is enough to assess the fit
props.body: You do not need a finished specification. The most useful inputs are the current problem, the target outcome, and the main delivery constraints.

The first contact does not need to be heavy. A practical summary of what is not working, what should change, and whether this is a new build, takeover, internal tool, or integration project is usually enough.

If the fit is strong, I will suggest the next step. If it is not, I will say that directly rather than stretch out the process.

If you already know the timeline, team setup, current stack, or operational risk, that context helps shape the conversation faster.
